title: The Case for Boring Index Funds in a Crypto World
excerpt: Crypto headlines dominate financial media, but the data on long-term wealth building still overwhelmingly favors low-cost, diversified index investing. Here's why boring wins, and where a small speculative allocation might still make sense for the right investor.
fullText:
It's easy to feel like you're missing out when timelines are full of crypto success stories. But survivorship bias is doing a lot of work in those stories — for every trader who turned a small stake into a fortune, there are far more who lost most of what they put in.

Index funds win over long horizons for a simple reason: they capture the average return of a broad market at very low cost, and they don't require you to be right about which asset, sector, or coin is going to outperform. Over 20-30 year horizons, that consistency compounds into real wealth far more reliably than concentrated bets.

That said, a small allocation to higher-risk, higher-volatility assets — crypto included — isn't irrational, provided it's sized so a total loss doesn't change your life. Many disciplined investors carve out 1-5% of a portfolio for this kind of speculation while keeping the core in diversified, low-cost funds. The mistake isn't holding crypto; it's holding it in place of a diversified core, or sizing it so large that a drawdown wrecks your plan.

The boring path — automate contributions to broad index funds, rebalance occasionally, ignore the noise — remains the highest-probability route to long-term financial independence for most people.

title: Michael Saylor's Infinite Money Glitch
excerpt: How MicroStrategy's Michael Saylor turned cheap corporate debt into a self-reinforcing Bitcoin acquisition machine — and the risk hiding underneath it.
fullText:
Michael Saylor, executive chairman of MicroStrategy, isn't just buying Bitcoin — he's reshaping corporate finance with it. By leveraging debt at incredibly low interest rates, sometimes less than 1%, Saylor is turning traditional financing into a Bitcoin acquisition machine.

The strategy revolves around a self-reinforcing cycle: stock prices rise, providing capital to buy more Bitcoin; Bitcoin prices rise, boosting MicroStrategy's value; repeat. With $14.8 billion in Bitcoin by early 2024, MicroStrategy has more than doubled its assets running this loop.

It's not without risk. A major price drop could cause significant losses, since the strategy amplifies gains and losses in both directions. Saylor has mitigated some of that risk by using non-callable loans and even repaying some debt with MicroStrategy stock instead of cash, protecting the company's actual Bitcoin holdings from a forced sale.

Saylor's approach is more than a bold investment move — it's proof of concept that with conviction and smart capital structuring, a public company can harness Bitcoin's volatility as a growth engine rather than a liability.

title: Bitcoin Bear Market Studies
excerpt: The Bitcoin bear market wasn't just a drawdown — it was a lesson in patience, conviction, and where wealth actually gets built.
fullText:
The Bitcoin bear market wasn't just a test — it was a lesson in resilience, strategy, and long-term thinking. A few takeaways from the downturn that still apply to navigating crypto with confidence.

Patience pays off. Markets move in cycles, and short-term fear often creates the exact setup for long-term opportunity. Conviction beats emotion — understanding Bitcoin's fundamentals is what lets you hold steady when prices drop instead of panic-selling at the bottom.

And the core lesson: wealth is built in the bear market. The best time to prepare for the next bull run is precisely when everyone else is scared and looking away. The bear market was tough, but it taught real lessons about investing, patience, and long-term vision — the kind you can only learn by living through a drawdown, not reading about one.

title: Bitcoin: The Key to Financial Freedom
excerpt: Scarcity, inflation resistance, and full custody of your own wealth — the three reasons Bitcoin keeps showing up as a long-term store of value.
fullText:
In a world of economic uncertainty, Bitcoin stands out as a powerful store of value. A few fundamentals explain why it keeps earning a place in long-term portfolios.

Scarcity is the foundation: with only 21 million Bitcoin that will ever exist, that hard cap drives long-term value in a way no fiat currency can replicate. It's also a real hedge against inflation — unlike fiat currencies, Bitcoin isn't subject to government manipulation or unlimited issuance.

And it's borderless and decentralized. Your wealth, your control, no middlemen needed to move it, hold it, or verify it. That combination — fixed supply, inflation resistance, and self-custody — is what separates it from every other asset class most people already own.

This isn't just an investment thesis, it's a financial revolution playing out in real time.

title: Unlock Business Success with Myers-Briggs
excerpt: Understanding your Myers-Briggs type can sharpen how you make decisions, lead a team, and play to your actual strengths as an entrepreneur.
fullText:
Have you ever wondered how your personality impacts your business decisions? Whether you're a crypto enthusiast or a seasoned investor, understanding your Myers-Briggs type can provide valuable insight for optimizing your entrepreneurial path.

The Myers-Briggs Type Indicator (MBTI) is a personality framework that categorizes individuals into 16 distinct types based on preferences like thinking versus feeling and introversion versus extraversion. Understanding your type gives you real insight into how you approach business challenges, collaborate with teams, and make decisions under pressure.

Every type has unique strengths. ENFPs, for example, are natural idea generators, while INTJs excel at strategic planning. Recognizing your strengths lets you focus on what you actually do best, turning those strengths into leverage instead of trying to force yourself into someone else's operating style.

How you respond to challenges often depends on your type, too. Extroverted types may thrive in networking and public speaking, while introverts tend to excel in deep, focused work. And successful businesses are built on strong teams — using Myers-Briggs to assess team dynamics can help you assemble a group that complements each other's strengths and weaknesses rather than duplicating them.

As David Brooks put it: "Wise people don't just possess information; they possess a compassionate understanding of other people. They know about life." Understanding yourself, and the people you build with, is still one of the highest-leverage things an entrepreneur can do.

title: $2.28M Luxury Design by Sherri Beier
excerpt: Step inside a $2.28M Minneapolis home where mid-century charm meets modern elegance, and hear the design story behind the transformation.
fullText:
Step inside one of the most breathtaking homes in Minneapolis — a $2.28M luxury property designed by interior designer Sherri Beier. This one's a look at the trends shaping high-end interior design right now, and how to bring some of those ideas into your own home.

Sherri takes us through the transformation of 2200 Drew Ave, where mid-century charm meets modern elegance by Cedar Lake. Her vision turned this property into a contemporary oasis in the heart of the Bryn Mawr neighborhood — the kind of renovation that respects the bones of a home while completely modernizing how it lives.

Designing a home like this takes real problem-solving: balancing period character against livability, sourcing materials that read as luxury without feeling cold, and sequencing a renovation so the house still functions along the way. Sherri Beier is an accomplished interior designer, realtor, and investor who blends design vision with practical functionality — proof that the best real estate plays combine an eye for design with an understanding of what actually adds value.

title: Cryptocurrency: The Next Global Reserve
excerpt: Inflation, geopolitical tension, and mistrust in fiat systems are pushing people toward decentralized alternatives. Here's the case for crypto as a global reserve asset.
fullText:
The financial world is on the brink of a revolution. Traditional currencies are being tested, and cryptocurrency is stepping in as a secure and efficient alternative. As decentralized systems grow in adoption, the case for cryptocurrency becoming the next global reserve currency becomes stronger.

Governments and central banks have long dominated the financial landscape, but cracks are forming. Inflation, geopolitical tensions, and mistrust in fiat systems are driving people to seek alternatives. Cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin offer a decentralized, borderless solution, free from manipulation — the dollar has no supply cap, while Bitcoin is fixed at 21 million.

Crypto isn't just a fad, it's a functional financial system. Transparency, immutability, and limited supply make it a robust contender for a global reserve currency. It's efficient for cross-border transactions, reduces costs, and removes the need for intermediaries.

Real-world adoption keeps building: El Salvador's Bitcoin adoption, corporate treasuries holding crypto, and growing institutional investment all signal a shift from niche interest to mainstream infrastructure. As adoption rises, traditional banking systems face real disruption, and the growth of stablecoins and DeFi pushes us closer to a financial ecosystem powered by blockchain rather than intermediaries.

As Milton Friedman put it: "Inflation is just like alcoholism; when you start printing too much money, the good effects come first, the bad effects only come later."

title: Top Altcoins to 100X in 2024!
excerpt: Bitcoin sets the tone, but altcoins tell the speculative story. Here are the catalysts — DeFi growth, Layer-2 scaling, and regulatory clarity — worth watching.
fullText:
Are you ready to uncover the hidden gems in the cryptocurrency market? From Bitcoin to Pepe, key catalysts, market trends, and recent news are all shaping the next moves in crypto.

Bitcoin remains the gold standard, setting the stage for market movements. With increasing institutional adoption and technological upgrades like the Lightning Network, Bitcoin continues to drive confidence across the market — everything else in crypto still trades relative to it.

Meme coins like Pepe are more than internet jokes — they're indicators of growing retail interest in crypto. While risky, they can offer outsized rewards during speculative market runs, for those sized to handle the downside.

A few catalysts worth watching heading into any altcoin cycle: DeFi growth, as decentralized finance platforms create opportunities for altcoins supporting lending, staking, and yield farming; Layer-2 solutions focused on scalability and speed gaining real traction; and regulatory clarity, as clearer government frameworks build investor confidence in altcoins broadly.

Stay updated on regulations, partnerships, and blockchain innovation — these are usually the actual triggers behind the next breakout, not just chart patterns. Strong fundamentals plus timing is still the whole game.

title: Crypto and Dedollarization: The Future of Money?
excerpt: Countries are gradually moving away from the U.S. dollar. Here's why cryptocurrency keeps coming up as the alternative — and what's still standing in its way.
fullText:
The global economy is witnessing a dramatic shift: countries are gradually moving away from their reliance on the U.S. dollar. This trend, known as dedollarization, has sparked a debate about what could replace the dollar as the world's reserve currency. Cryptocurrency, with its decentralized and borderless nature, is emerging as a viable alternative. But is it ready to take on the role?

Dedollarization refers to reducing the dollar's dominance in international trade and reserves. Countries like China, Russia, and members of BRICS are seeking alternatives to bypass reliance on the dollar for geopolitical and economic reasons.

Cryptocurrency offers real advantages over traditional fiat: its decentralized nature eliminates the need for middlemen and reduces transaction costs, while blockchain technology provides transparency and security that appeals to global financial systems.

Several forces are driving the shift — geopolitical tensions and sanctions push countries toward independence from the dollar, economies want to diversify reserves into gold, crypto, or other currencies, and inflation risk makes an asset like Bitcoin, with a hard cap, more attractive as a hedge.

Despite the momentum, crypto still has to clear real hurdles: regulatory uncertainty, volatility, and scalability, before it can function as a true global standard. Dedollarization is reshaping the global financial system, and while crypto's role as a reserve asset is still uncertain, one thing is clear — the way we think about money is changing.

title: Crypto Scams 101: Protect Your Investments
excerpt: Pump-and-dump schemes and influencer-driven promotions are rampant in crypto. Here's how to spot the tactics before they cost you money.
fullText:
The crypto market is filled with opportunities, but not all coins are created equal. Scams like pump-and-dump schemes and influencer-driven promotions are rampant. By understanding their tactics, you can avoid falling victim and make smarter investment decisions.

Not every coin gets hyped for its utility. Influencers often promote coins purely for financial gain, drawing buyers into pump-and-dump schemes while they quietly cash out on the way up. Projects like Bitcoin and Ethereum never rely on paid promotions — they're driven by real utility and community trust, which is exactly why they've survived multiple cycles while hype coins fade.

Scammers prey on hype. They launch coins with limited liquidity, driving prices up artificially before abandoning their holdings and leaving investors broke. These schemes happen daily, and increasingly involve bots and algorithms to manipulate thin order books.

Before investing, always research the team and their track record, verify the project's roadmap and actual community activity, and stay skeptical of heavy promotion or celebrity endorsements. In the volatile world of crypto, diligence is the whole game — established projects with strong communities and proven utility remain the safer bet.

title: Too Many Irons — But Is That Even Your Fire?
excerpt: What it means to have "too many irons in the fire" — and more importantly, how to know if that fire even belongs to you. Lessons from a bad business partnership and a messy artist-management deal.
fullText:
"Too many irons in the fire" is a saying I've heard for years. My buddy Malcolm reminds me of it often. He's not wrong — I juggle a lot. But I also love what I do, and I've built a skillset I can actually capitalize on.

One of my college professors once told me, "Sometimes having too many irons in the fire nets too many irons in the fire." I didn't fully get it back then, but I do now. If you've got too much going on, you might just end up spinning your wheels instead of making real progress. But still — I enjoy the grind. Having lots going on gives me room to move, to shift, to adapt.

Lately I've been asking myself: are the things I'm doing actually mine? Are they fueling my goals, my purpose? That's a question more people should ask themselves, frequently. You're not selfish for questioning whether your time and energy are being used well — you're being real. Because if the fire you're stoking doesn't lead to your dreams, maybe it's not your fire. Maybe someone else lit it, and you're just stuck feeding it.

Two examples from the field. First, a formal business partner: we ran a print-on-demand e-comm brand together. I trusted his experience and his ability to handle marketing. I was wrong, ended up funding everything, and learned the hard way that blind trust is expensive. Since then I check in, double-check, and triple-check. If you want something done right, do it yourself — or oversee it properly.

Second, an artist-management deal in LA. I started managing an artist I vibed with, and we signed an agreement quickly. I suddenly found myself doing way more than what was in scope — small things at first, like grabbing breakfast, but over time those "extras" clouded my judgment and diluted my focus. The lesson: set boundaries early, define scope clearly, and know what you bring to the table and what you're actually responsible for.

People often ask me what I do. My honest answer is: a lot. Photography, video, editing, drone work, real estate, investing, creative consulting. It sounds like a lot because it is a lot, but for me it works — multiple income streams mean multiple opportunities, and I thrive in that energy. If you've got a lot going on, embrace it. Let your fire burn. Just make sure it's your fire, and make sure it's pushing you forward.

title: Why Bitcoin Makes Sense in 2025 (And Beyond)
excerpt: Explore why Bitcoin still holds massive potential in 2025 — blockchain security, hard supply caps, liquidity, and where the four-year market cycle says we are right now.
fullText:
Bitcoin operates through the blockchain, a decentralized technology that manages and records transactions through advanced mathematical equations — not governments. A ledger on the blockchain functions like a real-time, interconnected digital account book, instantly recording price, asset details, and ownership. Because it's decentralized, no single entity can control or manipulate it — security is embedded in the code.

Unlike fiat currency, which governments can print endlessly, Bitcoin is capped at 21 million coins, ever. Around 18 million BTC have already been mined, with under 3 million remaining and the final coin projected around 2140. That fixed supply means no inflation from "printing more," no devaluation over time, and true scarcity driving long-term value.

Adoption keeps compounding: institutional flows, ETFs, and retail acceptance are all rising, yet Bitcoin's total market cap is still a tiny slice of global asset value. Market cap is simply price times circulating supply, so as demand rises against a fixed supply, price has to move — that's the entire thesis in one line.

Bitcoin also runs 24/7 with no middlemen or gatekeepers, giving you a level of liquidity and control that traditional markets like stocks or real estate can't match. And historically, it has moved in roughly four-year cycles — boom, correction, accumulation, final rise — repeating in 2013, 2017, and 2021.

Whether regulators ultimately classify it as a currency, a commodity, or a tech asset almost doesn't matter. That ambiguity is exactly what leaves room for continued growth and adoption. Bitcoin might feel volatile, but it's volatility with purpose — early-stage innovation always looks like this.
